response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile individual contributor with 15+ years of development experience and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, CI/CD infrastructure, IDE components, MCP code generators), but this is a Level II people-management role and the resume shows no people-management experience whatsoever. There is also no formal technical leadership role, no organizational change experience, and only incidental mobile-platform exposure, making the profile a poor fit for an engineering manager position despite solid technical depth.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, well above the 8-year minimum
Authentic developer-tools background: authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), built CI/CD and device-orchestration infrastructure, and contributed to IDE components and MCP code generators
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing inclination through workshops, multi-module courses, and coding dojos
concerns: No people-management experience at all, against a hard requirement of 2+ years for a management role
No evidence of formal technical leadership, roadmap ownership, or leading organizational change
Mobile-platform knowledge is dated and incidental (iOS/Apple TV work in 2015-2016) rather than ecosystem-level

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure background
